lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, my name is [player name]. Who are you?

lines.speaker: Fairy Aeryka
lines.text: Oh hello [player name]. I'm Aeryka. Aery for short.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Airy Fairy?

lines.speaker: Fairy Aeryka
lines.text: That's right. What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's that crop circle thing doing here?

lines.speaker: Fairy Aeryka
lines.text: Crop circle? Oh, you mean the Puromantic portal?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The pyromanic what?

lines.speaker: Fairy Aeryka
lines.text: The Puromantic portal. At least that's what we call them. It's the way the implings travel from Puro-Puro to other planes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Puro-Puro?

lines.speaker: Fairy Aeryka
lines.text: The impling home. We call it Puro-Puro. The implings just call it home, I think.

lines.speaker: Fairy Aeryka
lines.text: It's still here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pardon?

lines.speaker: Fairy Aeryka
lines.text: It's still here. The crop circle is still here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yes, thanks Aery. It didn't go anywhere in the meantime, then?

lines.speaker: Fairy Aeryka
lines.text: Nope. It just sat there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Jolly good. I can come back and visit Puro-Puro whenever I want then. Brilliant!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's in Puro-Puro?

lines.speaker: Fairy Aeryka
lines.text: Implings...and wheat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Erm, anything else?

lines.speaker: Fairy Aeryka
lines.text: Not really. Though I have noticed quite a lot of you humans travelling through the portal recently. I suppose you must like wheat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, most of us prefer lobsters to be perfectly honest, but there must be something interesting there.

lines.speaker: Fairy Aeryka
lines.text: Oh, I did notice a very serious-looking gnome go into the portal. Maybe he knows what's going on.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you remember what his name was?

lines.speaker: Fairy Aeryka
lines.text: Errm, Egg-nog, or something like that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, thanks! I'll have a chat with him when I go. Maybe he'll know what's going on.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what are these implings then?

lines.speaker: Fairy Aeryka
lines.text: Well, no-one knows for sure. The mischievous little creatures are probably related to imps. And they fly as well.

lines.speaker: Fairy Aeryka
lines.text: Also, like imps, they love collecting things. I'm not sure why, though. They also seem to like being chased.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So how would I get hold of what they are carrying, then?

lines.speaker: Fairy Aeryka
lines.text: Catch them, I suppose. I don't know really. Why would you want to?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, if they were carrying something useful. Maybe I could catch them with a big net - like butterflies.

lines.speaker: Fairy Aeryka
lines.text: Sounds a bit cruel to me, but I suppose that's possible.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've heard I may find dragon equipment in Puro-Puro.

lines.speaker: Fairy Aeryka
lines.text: Really? You humans like that stuff a lot, don't you? I don't like really old stuff myself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Old?

lines.speaker: Fairy Aeryka
lines.text: Yes, dragon stuff feels really old.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can you tell that?

lines.speaker: Fairy Aeryka
lines.text: From its magical aura, obviously. Oh, I forget you humans can't feel auras.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How old is old?

lines.speaker: Fairy Aeryka
lines.text: Really, really, old.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you be any more precise?

lines.speaker: Fairy Aeryka
lines.text: Not really. Time doesn't really mean a lot round here. Hundreds, maybe thousands of your human lifespans, I suppose. Anyway, it would have to be old since it all comes from the Necrosyrtes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Necrosyrtes? Who are they?

lines.speaker: Fairy Aeryka
lines.text: Old and powerful creatures. I don't think there have been any around here for aeons. I haven't seen one. I don't think they are very nice. Not like me. Anyway, this is all ancient history. Boooring!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: While I'm on the subject do you have any dragon stuff I can have?

lines.speaker: Fairy Aeryka
lines.text: Oh, I did have loads, but I threw it away.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Fairy Aeryka
lines.text: Only joking.

lines.speaker: Fairy Aeryka
lines.text: No, sorry, I can't help you there. You'll have to look for it yourself. Although, maybe if you find Necrosyrtes then they'll give you some. I heard they give dragon stuff away to people they like. Not fairies. We're too

lines.speaker: Fairy Aeryka
lines.text: nice.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, bye!

lines.speaker: Fairy Aeryka
lines.text: See you around!
